name: 2004 In Review: Apple — Part One (Year of the iPod, No 3-Peat on G5)
description: Macworld year-in-review recapping Apple's 2004: iPod division split from Mac, 2 million iPods sold in Q4 FY04 contributing to $276M annual profit, Power Mac G5 maxed at 2.5GHz (no 3GHz despite Jobs' mid-2003 promise), PowerBook lines upgraded to 1.5GHz with standard AirPort Extreme and Bluetooth. Peter Kastner (Aberdeen) explains the G5 90nm-transition wall as industry-wide, commending IBM's execution.
